channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Marvel Rivals The Thing and Human Torch release date
item.description: Here's when The Thing and Human Torch are being added toMarvel Rivals. We'll also give some of our predictions for how each character will work.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The cloud giants stumble
item.description: Recent financial results from the major cloud providers reveal a concerning trend in cloud adoption and growth. AWS reported just 13% growth in the final quarter of 2023, Microsoft Azure saw 19% growth, and Google Cloud achieved 26% growth—all below market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI shouldn’t accept Elon Musk’s $97 billion bid to buy it
item.description: Let’s say you own one of the most valuable homes in a lush, gated community that has been earmarked as a future point of growth for decades to come. One day, a letter appears in your mailbox, offering to buy your property for between a third and two-thirds of its value on the open market.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: IT unemployment hits new high as AI threat continues
item.description: Tech layoffs rose from 3.9% to 5.7% in one month, but it could just be that companies are faced with new year budgets.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Las decisiones estratégicas en IA de los CEO se ven aminoradas por la falta de conocimiento
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) continúa formando parte indiscutible del abecé de los directores ejecutivos y responsables de la toma de decisiones empresariales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nokia CEO Pekka Lundmark Steps Down, Justin Hotard Takes Over in April 2025
item.description: Nokia President and CEO Pekka Lundmark is stepping down after 4.5 years, with Intel Data Center & AI exec Justin Hotard set to replace him on April 1, 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Elon Musk is using tech to DOGE the government
item.description: Elon Musk is using his tech and leadership playbook in a crusade against the federal bureaucracy as he and his allies attempt to gain access to sensitive information and use emerging tech to speed up the process.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk calls for annual firing of judges after blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called for the annual firing of judges following an early Saturday decision from a judge stating that the Treasury Department should bar access to its payment systems to anyone besides “civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem defends Musk’s access to personal data
item.description: Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem defended tech billionaire Elon Musk’s access to sensitive data housed within DHS’s Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), saying he’s conducting a necessary “audit” of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Bill would ban DeepSeek
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Faxing in 2025: A Guide to Sending Faxes for Free
item.description: Discover how to send faxes for free using the HP Smart app, available on Android, iOS, Windows, and Mac. Learn about its features, security, and compliance with HIPAA regulations.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Beware of fake Reddit solutions delivering dangerous malware
item.description: Bad actors are now mimicking Reddit to spread malware that can steal personal information. CyberGuy shares what you need to know about fake Reddit pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hacked on social? Here’s what to do next
item.description: Spambots, data thieves, and other bad actors run rampant online — more so now than ever before. Here's what to do if one gets ahold of you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
